title: NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswalk control
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel or competent-authority review. Does not verify digital signatures on PDF readers outside Splunk—only fields present in ingested evidence metadata.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.56`, GRC dashboard workbook, ServiceNow task cross-links, and WORM/archival copies in `index=audit_evidence` with hash metadata attached.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An automated export lands in `audit_evidence` with `hash_verified=false` for `evidence_id=PACK-042` while the crosswalk lookup lacks any row for `nis2_clause=Art.24(placeholder)` so `iso27001_control` stays null; the search returns that evidence_id with scheduler_reason blank.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A fresh export arrives with matching `sha256_observed` and `sha256_expected`, `hash_verified=true`, a populated `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` row, and the related saved search completes without `scheduler` skips in the last 24 hours, producing zero rows when the alert runs.

dataSources: `index=audit_evidence` with `sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` or `sourcetype=evidence:export` (fields `evidence_id`, `nis2_clause`, `sha256_observed`, `sha256_expected`, `export_path`, `signed_by`, `hash_verified`). `index=_internal` `sourcetype=scheduler` for skipped or deferred saved searches (`savedsearch_name`, `status`, `reason`). Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) for attestation tickets. Lookup `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` columns `nis2_clause`, `iso27001_control`, `iec62443_requirement`, `nist_csf`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
(index=audit_evidence (sourcetype=evidence:saved_search OR sourcetype=evidence:export) earliest=-30d (hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR match(lower(validation_note),"(fail|mismatch|expired)"))) OR (index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-7d (status="skipped" OR status="deferred" OR like(reason,"%quota%")) savedsearch_name="UC-*")
| eval nis2_clause=coalesce(nis2_clause, obligation_clause, control_clause, "Art.21(2)(a)")
| lookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv nis2_clause OUTPUT iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, nist_csf
| eval crosswalk_ok=if(isnotnull(iso27001_control) AND isnotnull(iec62443_requirement),1,0)
| where crosswalk_ok=0 OR hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR status IN ("skipped","deferred")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen latest(reason) as scheduler_reason values(sourcetype) as evidence_channels values(export_path) as paths by evidence_id nis2_clause iso27001_control iec62443_requirement
| sort - last_seen
description: Detects evidence packages or scheduled compliance searches whose cryptographic verification failed, whose scheduler runs were skipped, or whose clause metadata cannot be mapped to the ISO/IEC crosswalk lookup.
value: Gives assurance teams a single pane to prove that each regulatory clause still chains to a recognised international control—and that the exported proof files were not altered after creation—before an external auditor asks for the mapping spreadsheet.
implementation: Emit structured JSON lines into `index=audit_evidence` whenever exports run; include `sha256_expected` from secure storage. Mirror saved-search health from `scheduler` (consider Splunk Monitoring Console). Maintain `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` under GRC change control. Install Splunkbase 1928 if attestation lives in ServiceNow. Route failures to Records & Information owner.
visualization: Table listing evidence_id, nis2_clause, iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, last_seen; sparkline of scheduler skips; single value for hash failures.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Alerts where nodename=Alerts.All_Alerts by Alerts.signature | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Optional Alerts acceleration if you fold notables into Enterprise Security; core UC runs fine on raw `audit_evidence` and `_internal`.

references.title: Splunk Docs: About saving searches and jobs

knownFalsePositives:
• **Manual PDF exports without hashes** — Distinguish: legacy workflow still valid until automation lands. Suppress: time-bound exception register rows tagged `manual_review_ok` with expiry.
• **Scheduler skips during indexer rolling restart** — Distinguish: short **5 minute** blip with `reason` mentioning bundle push. Suppress: filter SPL `where NOT match(reason,"bundle")` or require skips >3 per hour before alerting.
• **Clause aliases from national transposition** — Distinguish: local counsel cites subsection text while lookup expects high-level clause id. Suppress: extend `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` with alias column keyed via secondary lookup `nis2_clause_aliases.csv`.
• **ServiceNow catalog lag** — Distinguish: attestation task closed but REST poll not yet indexed. Suppress: increase Splunkbase 1928 input interval temporarily; join `snow:task` only when `sys_updated_on` < **15 minutes** lag.
• **Pen-test duplicate exports** — Distinguish: same evidence re-emitted hourly during purple-team exercise. Suppress: `dedup evidence_id` plus lookup `nis2_evidence_pen_test_runs.csv` listing approved export windows.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, GRC attestation task synchronisation)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC for evidence metadata, _internal for scheduler health)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, optional notable event aggregation and risk scoring)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `audit_evidence`, `_internal` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv')`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)(a)`. The control objective is: "Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.21(2)(a) using NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswalk control." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `audit_evidence`, `_internal` under `evidence:saved_search`, `evidence:export`, `scheduler`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


One operational consideration shapes the deployment of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Emit structured JSON lines into `index=audit_evidence` whenever exports run; include `sha256_expected` from secure storage. Mirror saved-search health from `scheduler` (consider Splunk Monitoring Console). Maintain `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` under GRC change control. Install Splunkbase 1928 if attestation lives in ServiceNow. Route failures to Records & Information owner. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswalk control," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This search enriches raw events with context from the lookup `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` — the enrichment table that adds compliance-relevant business context to raw events. The raw event stream alone cannot answer the compliance question because it does not carry the authoritative business attributes (`iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, nist_csf`) — these live in a register maintained outside the monitored system. The `| lookup` joins at search time on `nis2_clause`, making the search results only as accurate as the lookup's freshness.

Lookup ownership and freshness: This is the single most common failure mode for lookup-based compliance searches. The `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` table must be owned by a named team with a documented refresh cadence and staleness alerting. A minimum operational SLA is: (1) lookup refreshed at a cadence matching the risk domain (daily for access control, weekly for inventory, quarterly for vendor registers), (2) automated alert when the lookup has not been rewritten in 2x the refresh interval, (3) a row-count sanity check (alert if row count changes by more than 20% between refreshes — catches truncation bugs in upstream exports).

Case sensitivity: Splunk's lookup matching is case-sensitive by default. If the raw event field and the lookup key differ in case (e.g. `Account_Name=JSMITH` vs lookup `samaccountname=jsmith`), the join silently returns null. Normalize both sides with `| eval nis2_clause = lower(nis2_clause)` before the lookup, or set `case_sensitive_match = false` in the lookup's `transforms.conf` stanza. Search across the last 30 days.

**CIM / accelerated variant:** The detection also has an accelerated variant using `| tstats` against the CIM `Alerts` datamodel:

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Alerts where nodename=Alerts.All_Alerts by Alerts.signature | sort - count
```

When to prefer this variant: once the underlying datamodel is accelerated (verify in Settings > Data Models > `Alerts` > status=100%), this query runs 10-50x faster than the raw variant and can feasibly cover multi-month windows for audit reporting. When to avoid: if your field extractions are app-specific and the CIM tagging is incomplete, the `tstats` variant will silently under-count. Always compare raw vs accelerated counts on a known day before swapping the production schedule. Maintain both variants in the UC — the raw is the primary for accuracy, the accelerated is the performance optimisation for dashboarding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.56: NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswa]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.56,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `evidence_id`, `nis2_clause`, `hash_verified`, `iso27001_control`, `last_seen`. Run:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table evidence_id, nis2_clause, hash_verified, iso27001_control, last_seen
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.56: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Listing Evidence Id (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Nis2 Clause (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Iso27001 Control (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 4 — Iec62443 Requirement (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 5 — Last Seen; Sparkline Of Scheduler Skips; Single Value For Hash Failures (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** Check the lookup's case sensitivity (Splunk lookups are case-sensitive by default) and null handling — a null on the input side produces a silent miss, not an error.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure the proof files you hand auditors still match what the system first produced, and that each legal phrase lines up with the international safety-and-security checklists people expect. When something is missing, renamed, or skipped by the nightly robot, we call it out early.
